1_Glossary.definition: The final product or service the customer expects; represents the unit of value delivered to the client.
A digital “card” that travels with every job, holding status, sensitivity, fragility and processing history; single source of truth about the job’s progress.
A specific, partial activity that must be performed on a job to move it toward completion; a job can contain one or more tasks.

2.1_VisionAndGoals: Build an MVP platform – Deliver the minimum‑viable platform that enables orchestration of work between human operators and intelligent agents.

Validate the three‑layer architecture – Prove the proposed architecture (Inspection Center, Work‑Center, Agent Lab, Tool Lab, Core Services and Supervisor Hub) in a production pilot with limited functionality.
Gather feedback – Collect input from users and stakeholders on the basic functions to refine requirements for later phases.
Define scope boundaries – Identify what features are in the MVP and what will be left for future expansion, helping control the scope of later phases.

2.2.1_ThreeLayerArchitecture.diagram:
┌─────────────────────────────┐   99th Floor — Supervisor Hub
│   0th Floor — Production    │   Work‑Center • Inspection Center
│  −1st Floor — Development   │   Agent Lab • Tool Lab
└─────────────────────────────┘               │
                                       Core Services
2.2.1_ThreeLayerArchitecture.99thFloor_SupervisorHub_1.0: Supervisory and administrative console. Shows real‑time metrics (active tasks, agent vs. human success, processing times), detailed Control Records, and lets supervisors intervene (reassign jobs, adjust parameters).

0thFloor_ShopFloor.WorkCenter_1.0: Environment where agents process work tasks; handles job assignment and processing according to the selected mode.
0thFloor_ShopFloor.InspectionCenter_1.0: Dedicated work‑center (non‑modifiable) that reviews incoming jobs and routes them to the proper Work‑Center.
0thFloor_ShopFloor.CoreServices_1.0: Workflow orchestration, Control Record management, security (authN/authZ), data storage and shared utilities. Guided Rails moves jobs and data between components.

−1stFloor_Workshop.AgentLab_1.0: Development and runtime environment for intelligent agents: define agent logic, integrate tools from Tool Lab, deploy to production.
−1stFloor_Workshop.ToolLab_1.0: Library and management of tools (APIs, database queries, etc.) that agents can use; supports versioning and secure secrets storage.

guiding_principles: Iterative delivery – Build a minimal core first, then expand and tune in short cycles.
Early integration – Connect all components via Guided Rails already in the MVP to prove end‑to‑end flow.
Modularity – Each component has clear interfaces, making later replacement or extension easy.
Limited scope – Only must‑have features are in the MVP; advanced functions and optimisations are deferred.

2.2.2_ControlRecordDataModel: Link to files in WIP storage

2.2.3_GuidedRailsOrchestration.Intake: Job without a Control Record → Inspection Center
2.2.3_GuidedRailsOrchestration.Classification: Add functional pattern and asset type

Routing.no_match: enqueue in Pending until configuration is extended

2.2.3_GuidedRailsOrchestration.Completion: Control Record closes; result returned to requester

2.3_RolesAndResponsibilities: Agent Developer (Agent Lab user) – Designs and configures intelligent agents, selects or creates tools, tunes behaviour.
Supervisor (Supervisor Hub user) – Monitors platform activity, user workload and agent performance; intervenes when needed.
Platform Administrator – Maintains Core Services, infrastructure, security and background configuration. (In small teams the supervisor may take this role.)
2.4_NonFunctionalMVPRequirements: Modularity – Each module is an independent service with a clear API.
Security – OIDC SSO, RBAC/ABAC, secrets managed in Vault.
Observability – Central logs, metrics and alerts visible in Supervisor Hub.
Extensibility – New Work‑Centers or tools can be added without redeploying the core.

2.5_ModuleCatalog_Phase1.purpose: Governs job flow via Guided Rails, stores lifecycle in the Control Record, acts as secure gateway to external APIs and collects metrics.
Entry gate: enriches new jobs with asset type, functional pattern and confidence score so they enter production correctly classified.
Hosts a specific Work‑Agent that processes jobs autonomously (Auto‑pilot) or semi‑autonomously (Co‑pilot / Manual) and records everything in the Control Record.
Real‑time dashboards, alerts and approval UI. Shows progress, automation rate and LLM costs; lets supervisors handle exceptions instantly.
Low‑code workshop for configuring digital colleagues: behaviour archetype, tools, memory, autonomy level. One‑click deploy to prod after sandbox testing.
Securely manages internal and external tools (APIs, models, databases); stores secrets in Vault; logs usage; supports hot updates.
DevOps console: GitOps config, CI/CD pipelines, backups. Enables rapid creation of new environments (test → stage → prod) and safe module upgrades.
End‑to‑end identity (SSO/OIDC), RBAC/ABAC enforced by OPA, secrets rotation, central logging to SIEM, anomaly detection and audits (GDPR & ISO 27001).

3.1_ConfigureAndViewAnAgent: Modules in action: Security → Supervisor Hub → Agent Lab ↔ Core Services (+ Tool Lab for tool list)
R&D Engineer logs in via Security 1.0.
In Supervisor Hub (landing page) selects Agent Lab → Core verifies permission and redirects.
Agent Lab fetches the list of agents, their states and hosting Work‑Centers from Core and shows Agents & Allocation.
Engineer clicks an agent to see archetype, autonomy level, assigned tools, LLM limits, memory config.
If authorised, they edit (e.g. switch from Co‑pilot to Auto‑pilot) → Agent Lab sends a PATCH; Core versions the config and restarts the Work‑Agent.
Sandbox test can be run – Agent Lab spins up a temporary Work‑Center with synthetic data; results appear in a Control Record preview.
On success, the engineer deploys to production; Core removes sandbox queues and places the agent in the live Work‑Center.
3.2_ProcessAReceiptConversionRequest: Modules in action: Core Services → Inspection Center → Core → Work‑Center (+ Tool Lab) → Core → Supervisor Hub → Core
External Task Manager sends a new request with the receipt file to Core. Core creates a Control Record (state: New) and stores the file in WIP.
Guided Rails sees no Control Record → sends job to Inspection Center.
Decision Scientist Agent in Inspection Center classifies functional pattern ASSESS, asset Generic Artefact, calculates confidence, writes data to the Control Record, returns the job to Core.
Core checks for a Work‑Center registered for ASSESS + Generic Artefact: if exists → enqueue; if not → Control Record state Pending – no WC; job appears in supervisor queue.
Work‑Agent in Work‑Center pulls Control Record and WIP file, calls OCR and extraction tools from Tool Lab, logs steps and final result to Control Record.
Result handling: Auto‑pilot → Core marks job Done and publishes the output; Co‑pilot / Manual → Core creates a task in Supervisor Hub (state Waiting for Approval).
Supervisor reviews the result and clicks Approve; Core closes the Control Record and notifies the requester.
3.3_SupervisionAndPlatformManagement: Modules in action: Security → Supervisor Hub ↔ Core + Administration (CI/CD) + Security (RBAC, alerts)
Platform administrator logs in (SSO) and opens Admin Dashboard in Supervisor Hub.
Unassigned Requests panel auto‑loads all Control Records flagged Pending – no WC and shows asset‑pattern combos without a Work‑Center.
Admin can create a new Work‑Center (Administration → CI/CD → Core registers queues) or manually assign an existing job to another Work‑Center.
Dashboard shows KPIs (live jobs, average processing time, automation rate, LLM cost) sourced from Core metrics and Security logs.
If a KPI breaches a threshold (e.g. > 5 % agent errors), Supervisor Hub highlights an alert and sends Slack/e‑mail notifications.
From the same UI the admin can roll back a release, restart a Work‑Center or update an RBAC policy.

4.1_ManagementAndSupportCapabilities: DIRECT – Define strategy, goals and policies for a unit (e.g. divisional management).
MANAGE – Oversee day‑to‑day operations, assign work, solve operational issues (e.g. branch management).
ADMINISTER – Handle administration: time sheets, costs, revenue tracking.
OPERATE – Run equipment or highly‑automated environments (e.g. intranet infrastructure).
PROCESS – Execute tasks by a fixed procedure, typically back‑office (e.g. finalising customer offers).
4.2_ResourceManagement: REGISTER – Capture and maintain reference data on an entity (e.g. customer directory).
DESIGN – Create or update a design of a product, service or model.
DEVELOP – Develop or enhance an IT system, including testing and deployment.
ASSESS – Test or verify an entity against a formal norm or regulation.
MAINTAIN – Perform maintenance and repairs on services or equipment.
4.3_ActivityOversight: TRACK – Keep a running log of transactions or activities for later analysis.
ANALYSE – Examine behaviour or performance of ongoing activity.
MONITOR – Observe and evaluate an entity’s state or metrics in real time.
